title: Stripe API — Payment Intents
overview: Subset of the official Stripe OpenAPI spec (spec3.sdk.json) covering the core Payment Intents lifecycle: create, retrieve, update, confirm, cancel, capture. Stripe is a REST API; request bodies are application/x-www-form-urlencoded and successful responses return the PaymentIntent object. Authentication uses the secret API key via HTTP Bearer (or HTTP Basic with the key as username).

security_schemes.name: bearerAuth
security_schemes.details: HTTP Bearer authentication: Authorization: Bearer <secret API key>.

security_schemes.name: basicAuth
security_schemes.details: HTTP Basic authentication: the secret API key as the username, password empty.

schemas.name: PaymentIntent

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the object.

fields.name: object
fields.description: String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Amount intended to be collected by this PaymentIntent. A positive integer representing how much to charge in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal) (e.g., 100 cents to charge $1.00

fields.name: amount_received
fields.description: Amount that this PaymentIntent collects.

fields.name: currency
fields.description: Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies).

fields.name: status
fields.description: Status of this PaymentIntent, one of `requires_payment_method`, `requires_confirmation`, `requires_action`, `processing`, `requires_capture`, `canceled`, or `succeeded`. Read more about each PaymentIntent [status](https:

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description: The client secret of this PaymentIntent. Used for client-side retrieval using a publishable key.   The client secret can be used to complete a payment from your frontend. It should not be stored, logged, or exposed to an

fields.name: customer
fields.description: ID of the Customer this PaymentIntent belongs to, if one exists.  Payment methods attached to other Customers cannot be used with this PaymentIntent.  If [setup_future_usage](https://api.stripe.com#payment_intent_object-

fields.name: payment_method
fields.description: ID of the payment method used in this PaymentIntent.

fields.name: payment_method_types
fields.description: The list of payment method types (e.g. card) that this PaymentIntent is allowed to use. A comprehensive list of valid payment method types can be found [here](https://docs.stripe.com/api/payment_methods/object#payment_me

fields.name: capture_method
fields.description: Controls when the funds will be captured from the customer's account.

fields.name: confirmation_method
fields.description: Describes whether we can confirm this PaymentIntent automatically, or if it requires customer action to confirm the payment.

fields.name: created
fields.description: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

fields.name: description
fields.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users.

fields.name: latest_charge
fields.description: ID of the latest [Charge object](https://docs.stripe.com/api/charges) created by this PaymentIntent. This property is `null` until PaymentIntent confirmation is attempted.

fields.name: next_action
fields.description: If present, this property tells you what actions you need to take in order for your customer to fulfill a payment using the provided source.

fields.name: receipt_email
fields.description: Email address that the receipt for the resulting payment will be sent to. If `receipt_email` is specified for a payment in live mode, a receipt will be sent regardless of your [email settings](https://dashboard.stripe.co

fields.name: setup_future_usage
fields.description: Indicates that you intend to make future payments with this PaymentIntent's payment method.  If you provide a Customer with the PaymentIntent, you can use this parameter to [attach the payment method](/payments/save-duri

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Learn more about [storing in

fields.name: livemode
fields.description: If the object exists in live mode, the value is `true`. If the object exists in test mode, the value is `false`.

schemas.constraints: A PaymentIntent guides you through the process of collecting a payment from your customer. We recommend that you create exactly one PaymentIntent for each order or customer session in your system. You can reference the P

operational.detail: Authenticate with your secret API key via HTTP Bearer (Authorization: Bearer sk_...) or HTTP Basic (key as username).
Request bodies are application/x-www-form-urlencoded; nested params use Stripe's bracket syntax (e.g. metadata[key]=value).
Per-operation error responses are not enumerated inline in the spec subset; Stripe returns a standard error object with an HTTP 4xx status. Not captured here.

missing: Per-operation error responses / status codes are not captured in this subset (Stripe uses a global error object schema).
The PaymentIntent schema has ~45 properties; only ~20 representative core fields are captured here.
Nested request objects (automatic_payment_methods, payment_method_data, shipping, transfer_data, etc.) are captured by name/type only, not their full sub-schemas.
